name: Planet Labs PBC

profile.description: Planet Labs PBC operates the largest constellation of Earth-imaging satellites, providing daily imagery and geospatial data to agriculture, government, forestry, and intelligence customers. Public Benefit Corporation focused on using space data to benefit humanity.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): Planet operates 200+ satellites providing daily global imagery — an unprecedented capability. Revenue grew 42% YoY. AI Deployment (2/2): AI-powered analytics for crop monitoring, deforestation detection, and defense intelligence with measurable customer value. Market Share (1/2): Largest commercial Earth observation constellation. Product Pipeline (1/2): Expanding AI analytics capabilities and next-gen satellites. R&D Intensity (1/1): High R&D intensity for satellite/AI company.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): Public Benefit Corporation using space data to benefit humanity — genuine purpose validated by PBC structure. Community Impact (1/2): Open data initiatives for climate research. Employee Retention (0/2): No data. Insider Buying (1/2): Founder maintains stake.

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored. D/E of 110% (score 0.83). ROE deeply negative at -84% (score 0.0, 2x weight). Revenue growth of 42.1% excellent (score 1.0). Profit margin of -111% (score 0.0). Forward P/E negative (excluded).

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (7/9): Satellite constellation is physical infrastructure AI cannot replicate. Earth observation data is proprietary and requires space assets. AI Opportunity (1/1): AI analytics on satellite imagery is a core revenue driver, with quantifiable customer applications.

infinite_game.summary: Planet Labs as a Public Benefit Corporation shows genuine infinite game thinking — using space data to benefit humanity. Founder Will Marshall has maintained this vision since founding.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): PBC structure formalizes the purpose of using space data to benefit humanity. Validated by consistent execution.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): Stable founder-led organization with mission-driven culture.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): Acknowledges Maxar and Airbus as motivation to improve revisit rate and AI capabilities.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): Pivoted from cubesat experiments to commercial Earth observation platform.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): Built satellite constellation as a PBC rather than optimizing for pure profit.

investment_thesis: Planet Labs delivered 42% revenue growth powered by AI analytics on the world's largest Earth observation constellation. PBC structure aligns purpose with profit. Government defense and intelligence contracts provide recurring revenue.
thesis_continued: Deeply unprofitable with -111% profit margin and -84% ROE. D/E of 110% with no profitability timeline. $11.5B market cap on pre-profit fundamentals. Competition from larger players (Maxar/Airbus) and government satellites.
key_catalysts: Government defense contract expansion for AI-powered geospatial intelligence
Path to profitability through scale and AI analytics revenue growth
Next-gen satellite constellation improving imagery resolution and revisit rate
key_risks: Deeply negative profitability (-111% margin, -84% ROE) with no clear timeline to breakeven
Competition from better-funded satellite operators Maxar and Airbus
$11.5B market cap on pre-profit fundamentals creates severe downside risk
sources: Planet Labs FY2026 Results — SEC Filings, Yahoo Finance, 2026
